summary: Restoration playwright. Known for The Way of the World (Mirabell and Millamant's 'proviso scene'), Love for Love, and The Mourning Bride (source of 'music has charms to soothe a savage breast' and 'hell hath no fury like a woman scorned').

works.name: The Way of the World
works.description: Comedy featuring the lovers Mirabell and Millamant.

clues.clue: Mirabell and Millamant; famous 'proviso scene' laying out marriage conditions
examples: Mirabell foiling a blackmail plot by Fainall and marrying Millamant
two lovers discuss the use of masks... when laying out the terms of their marriage

clues.clue: Lady Wishfort; Fainall's blackmail foiled when Mrs. Fainall's fortune already belongs to Mirabell
examples: Mrs. Fainall's fortune already belongs to Mirabell

clues.clue: Waitwell pretends to be Sir Rowland to trick Lady Wishfort
examples: Waitwell pretends to be Sir Rowland

works.name: Love for Love
works.description: Valentine feigns madness; Angelica tests devotion by pretending to love Sir Sampson.

clues.clue: Valentine's brother fakes madness; Angelica pretends to love Sir Sampson to test Valentine
examples: Angelica reveals that she was pretending to love Sir Sampson Legend only to test the devotion of his son Valentine

works.name: The Mourning Bride
works.description: Source of famous quotations. Zara poisons herself.

clues.clue: 'Music has charms to soothe a savage breast'
examples: Music has charms to soothe a savage breast

clues.clue: Paraphrased as 'hell hath no fury like a woman scorned'
examples: hell hath no fury like a woman scorned

clues.clue: Zara imbibes poison mistakenly; Almeria and Alphonso's love
examples: Zara imbibes poison, mistakenly believing she dies beside Osmyn

comprehensive_summary: William Congreve was a major Restoration playwright. The Way of the World features Mirabell and Millamant's famous 'proviso scene.' Love for Love has Valentine feigning madness. The Mourning Bride is the source of 'music has charms to soothe a savage breast' and 'hell hath no fury like a woman scorned.' He was a member of the Kit-Cat Club and friend of Jonathan Swift.

cards.front: Play: Mirabell and Millamant negotiate marriage in the 'proviso scene'; Fainall's blackmail foiled
cards.back: The Way of the World (William Congreve)

cards.front: Play: Valentine feigns madness; Angelica pretends to love Sir Sampson to test Valentine's devotion
Play: source of 'music has charms to soothe a savage breast' and 'hell hath no fury like a woman scorned'
